# Reeloo Unification — 5 Apps → 1 System

**Design brief for Claude design**
**Author:** orchestrator (claw on minis)
**Date:** 2026-05-19
**Status:** v1.0 — ready to consume
**Target system:** `reeloo.ai` (public) + `internal.reeloo.ai` (workshop)

---

## 0. Executive summary (1 page)

There are five language-learning surfaces in this fleet today. They all share the same user (one person — adult B1/B2 German + N5 Japanese learner in Switzerland), the same dictionary, the same TTS pipeline, the same audio assets in GCS, and the same north star (*save human time on non-creative work*). They differ in repo, port, framework version, design tokens, and brand voice. This brief asks the design team to converge them — visually first, then structurally — into one coherent product without erasing the differentiator each app earned.

The five surfaces:

| App | Where it lives | Live URL | Earned moat |
|---|---|---|---|
| **podcast-app** | `~/work/podcast-app/` (Next 16) | `podcast.reeloo.ai` | KarottenPlayerV3 audio player; on-demand wizard dialogue generation |
| **learn-stations** | `~/work/learn-stations/` (Next 16) | drives depot `/learn/*` | Station-based drill engine, WCAG-locked theme system, zod-typed exercises |
| **flashcard** | `~/work/notes/app/practice/flashcards/` (Next 16, inside depot) | `/practice/flashcards` | LoRA-illustrated dictionary cards + connection to live dictionary API |
| **readalong** | `~/work/readalong/nextjs/` (Next, "drill-theme" — teal sage) + `~/work/notes/app/podcast/_components/` (Karotten player twin) | depot routes `/podcast/*`, `/readalong` legacy | Audio-aligned text with word-level highlighting |
| **book-app** | `~/work/book-app/` (Next 16) | `book.reeloo.ai` (Vercel) | EPUB ingestion, Plex Serif typography, deck-mode single-sentence study |

The core problem: every product re-invented its own audio button, its own "correct/wrong" green/red, its own toolbar, its own dictionary popover, its own progress indicator. The user has gone on record (>10× in memory) that TTS must be Google-Wavenet quality, STT must auto-stop, and Chinese must be zh-TW. Those rules are non-negotiable and apply across the unified surface.

**The recommendation: a *hybrid* — single design system across all five, but keep two binaries.**

1. **Public binary = monorepo at `~/work/notes/`** (the existing depot app) — absorbs podcast-app, learn-stations, flashcard, readalong as path-based products under `reeloo.ai/{podcast,read,learn,tutorial}`. This matches the LOCKED four-product split in memory (`project_four_products_architecture.md`).
2. **Reader binary stays separate = `book-app`** at `book.reeloo.ai` — its EPUB ingestion + page-mode + deck-mode reader are deep enough that splitting them off keeps the depot app fast. Book-app *consumes* the shared design system as an npm-linked package.

Why hybrid not full-merge: book-app earned its `Plex Serif 19px / line-height 1.75` reading rhythm; collapsing it back into the depot Tailwind soup will regress. Why hybrid not full-split: podcast / learn / flashcard / readalong share so much data and so many components that keeping them in separate repos is what got us here. Two binaries, one design system, one dictionary, one auth, one TTS endpoint.

**What we ask design for:**

1. A complete token JSON file (`tokens.json`) covering colors / type / spacing / radius / shadow / motion across **4 themes** (solarized-light, solarized-dark, paper, ink) — solarized-light is the floor since both learn-stations and book-app already pass WCAG AA on it.
2. Mockups for 18 screens (listed in §7) at desktop + mobile widths.
3. One **shared component library** spec for the 16 primitives in §5 with named CSS variables and example HTML/JSX.
4. A migration map — for each existing surface, what changes / what stays.
5. **OUT of scope:** marketing site, payment flow, admin/back-office, agent fleet dashboards, the wizard-generation form (it's a separate workshop concern).

---

## 1. Per-app survey

### 1.1 podcast-app — `~/work/podcast-app/`

**Stack.** Next.js 16.2.2, React 19.2.4, Tailwind 4, TypeScript strict. Vitest + Playwright. Vercel-deployed to `podcast.reeloo.ai`.

**Entry routes.**
- `/` — catalog of baked demo episodes grouped by language
- `/podcast/[slug]` — hero player (KarottenPlayerV3) with synced transcript + keyboard a11y
- `/wizard` — on-demand dialogue generation form (~45–60s wait)
- `/api/wizard` — server-side proxy to depot `/api/wizard/generate`

**Data.** `data/demos/*.json` (baked transcripts), `data/podcast-recommendations.json`. Backend: Cloud Run `reeloo-whisper` (on `depot-493616`), GCS buckets `reeloo-podcast-audio` + `reeloo-podcast-transcripts`, depot `/api/wizard/generate`.

**Tokens (extracted from `globals.css`).** Minimal — page bg `#0f1419`, fg `#e8eef5`, no design tokens at the page level. *All real styling lives inside `KarottenPlayerV3.tsx`* as a scoped style block: brand color `#ff6f3c` (Karotten orange), accent `#1f6feb`, neutral greys with deliberate `kt3-*` prefix. Font: `--kt3-font-sans-loaded` (Inter via next/font).

**Components.** `KarottenPlayerV3` (hero card), `KarottenVideoPlayer`, `PodcastPlayerV1`, `PodcastPlayerV3`, `PlayerSwitcher`, `CatalogClient`, `RecentRow`, `ProgressModal`.

**Key flow.** User opens `/`, picks an episode card, hero player loads at `/podcast/[slug]`. Audio + scrolling karaoke transcript; keyboard arrows scrub. Wizard flow: type "level + topic + 2 voices" → wait → episode appears at `/podcast/[wizard-slug]`.

**Brand voice.** Bold, dark mode, orange accent, big play button. "Listen to learn." Player is the hero.

### 1.2 learn-stations — `~/work/learn-stations/`

**Stack.** Next.js 16.2.4, React 19.2.4, Tailwind 4, TypeScript strict, zod 4, kuromoji (JP), `microsoft-cognitiveservices-speech-sdk` (TTS), next-auth 5. Port 3210 in dev. Drives depot routes `/learn/minna`, `/learn/mundart`, `/learn/deutsch`, etc.

**Entry routes.**
- `/` — language picker
- `/[course]` — course landing (e.g. `/minna`, `/mundart`)
- `/cards` — card-deck mode
- `/exp3` — pattern-stack drill experiment
- `/api/tts`, `/api/dictionary-proxy`, `/api/learning-event`

**Data.** `data/courses/*.json` (course manifests), `data/syllabus/*` (unit-by-unit), `data/cards-state/` (SRS state), `data/learning-events.jsonl` (event log). Consumes shared dictionary at `dictionary.reeloo.ai`.

**Tokens (extracted from `globals.css`).** This is the **most mature token system** in the fleet. Solarized-light default + locked WCAG AA across 4 themes. Variables include:
- `--surface`, `--surface-container`, `--surface-elevated` (M3-style layered surfaces)
- `--correct-bg/fg/ring`, `--wrong-bg/fg/ring`, `--warning-bg/fg/ring` (graded-state tokens with documented contrast ratios)
- `--pos-place`, `--pos-subject`, `--pos-object`, `--pos-verb`, `--pos-particle` (POS coloring — orange/blue/magenta/green/grey)
- `--tts-highlight-bg/fg/ring` (TTS karaoke word highlight)
- Solarized palette: base3 `#fdf6e3`, base02 `#073642`, blue `#268bd2`, green `#859900` (and a darker `#6b7a00` for AA on serif), orange `#cb4b16`, magenta `#d33682`, yellow `#b58900`, violet `#6c71c4`

There is also a `lib/design-tokens.test.ts` that fails the build if any pair drops below 4.5:1. **This is the foundation the unified system should adopt.**

**Components.** `Station.tsx` (10 station variants), `Scene.tsx`, `Mascot.tsx`, `PlayTTSButton`, `PlaySlowTTSButton`, `RubyText` (JP furigana), `JpPosColored`, `JaText`, `EtymologyBubble`, `BottomCTA`, `AutoAdvancePill`, `DrillDensityBadge`, `TierPicker`, `FlagIssue`, `STTDebugPanel`, `TTSDebugPanel`, `SettingsButton`.

**Key flow.** Pick course → pick unit → drill through 8–12 stations (multi-choice, match, type-in, pronounce, listen-and-pick, etc.). Each station has a `BottomCTA` that goes green when correct, red when wrong. Auto-advances 1.2 s after correct. Auto-stop STT on silence.

**Brand voice.** Solarized parchment. Calm, generous whitespace, mascot in corner, type-heavy, *not* gamified — closer to a textbook than to Duolingo.

### 1.3 flashcard — `~/work/notes/app/practice/flashcards/`

**Stack.** Inside the depot monorepo (Next.js 16, the notes app). Client component (`'use client'`), no per-app tokens.

**Entry route.** `/practice/flashcards`

**Data.** `GET /api/dictionary-illustration/random?lang=<x>` returns `{ word, lang, illustration_url, entry }`. Entry uses the shared dictionary schema (senses, examples, readings, partOfSpeech, level). Illustrations are LoRA-trained Flux outputs at `/api/dictionary-illustration?word=…`.

**Components.** None reusable yet — single-page tap-to-flip card. Inherits depot's globals.css theme (`--bg`, `--bg-card`, `--text`, `--accent`).

**Key flow.** Pick language (ja/de/en) → see word + illustration → tap "Show meaning" → see definition + example → "Next" for another random card. No SRS yet (placeholder for `Saw it / Knew it / Skip`).

**Brand voice.** Quiet. Illustration-led. The LoRA art is the personality.

### 1.4 readalong — `~/work/readalong/nextjs/` + depot `/podcast/_components/`

**This one is split across two trees** — historically a Vite SPA at `~/work/readalong/nextjs/` (despite the path, the *runtime* uses both vite scripts and a nextjs subtree), and the Karotten-style player which was extracted into `notes/app/podcast/_components/`. The "drill" portion at `~/work/readalong/nextjs/src/app/drill/` is the canonical readalong UI today, with its own teal-sage **"drill theme"**.

**Stack.** Mixed: the vite SPA uses React 18, but the nextjs subtree uses Next/React 19. Tailwind 3 (older) with `class`-based dark mode + `tailwindcss-animate`. Firebase auth + storage for legacy decks. ffmpeg-wasm.

**Entry routes (`/nextjs/src/app/`).** `/`, `/about`, `/browse`, `/daily-review`, `/dashboard`, `/deck`, `/demo`, `/dictionary`, `/discover`, `/drill`, `/favorites`, `/game`, `/history`, `/intro`, `/library`, `/library-new`, `/map`, `/owned`, `/playlist`, `/profile`, `/scenario-sim`, `/statistics`, `/admin`. Many of these are dead routes.

**Data.** Per-deck JSON in `ai-decks/`, `annotations/`, Firebase user state. Audio in GCS.

**Tokens (extracted from `styles/drill-theme.css`).** Teal/sage palette, HSL-based:
- bg gradient `hsl(30, 30%, 92%) → hsl(40, 35%, 88%)` (warm cream)
- accent `hsl(170, 45%, 35%)` (teal)
- success `hsl(142, 71%, 40%)`
- error `hsl(0, 65%, 50%)`
- card shadow `0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`
- separate `--drill-chip-bg`, `--drill-progress-fill` (linear gradient)
This is a *different* palette from learn-stations' solarized. The visual rhythm is also different — warmer creams, soft glassy cards instead of M3 surfaces.

**Components.** Word-chips, drop-zone, drill card, progress bar, recording-dot. ShadCN-style scaffolding (`hsl(var(--primary))`) — the only app using it.

**Key flow.** Upload a text (or pick a deck) → segment into sentences → for each sentence, hear-and-repeat with auto-segmentation, then word-level matching drill. ffmpeg-wasm does in-browser audio splitting.

**Brand voice.** Pastel, warm, glassy. The aesthetic the user (memory: "Readalong drill patterns repeated 10x") loves and wants preserved as a *mode* of the unified drill engine.

### 1.5 book-app — `~/work/book-app/`

**Stack.** Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind 4, TypeScript. `epub2` for EPUB ingestion. `flexsearch` for in-book search. Vertex AI for annotation. Microsoft Cognitive Services for TTS. Vercel-deployed to `book.reeloo.ai`.

**Entry routes.**
- `/` — library shelf + drop-zone
- `/read/[bookId]` — chapter reader (page mode + deck mode)
- `/search` — global search across books

**Data.** `data/books/<id>/` — chapters.json, sentences.json, annotations.json, search-index.json. EPUB stored, ingested once.

**Tokens (extracted from `globals.css`).** Annotation underline tokens:
- `--anno-vocab: #268bd2` (blue, solid 2px)
- `--anno-grammar: #859900` (green, solid 2px)
- `--anno-idiom: #6c71c4` (violet, solid 2px)
- `--anno-place: #cb4b16` (orange, solid 2px)
- `--anno-archaism: #b58900` (yellow, dotted 2px)
- `--anno-proper-noun: #268bd2` (blue, solid 2px)

Reading rhythm tokens:
- `--reading-line-height: 1.75`
- `--reading-max-width: 720px`
- `--font-serif: var(--font-plex-serif)` (Plex Serif)
- `--font-sans: var(--font-plex-sans)` (Plex Sans)

Mode-specific motion: `@keyframes deck-in` (translateY 8px, 460 ms ease-out).

**Components.** `BookCard`, `ChapterTOC`, `ChapterNav`, `ChapterReader` (page mode), `DeckReader` (deck mode), `PageReader`, `AnnotationPopover`, `AnnotationToggle`, `LanguageToggle`, `ModeToggle`, `DropZone`, `SearchBox`, plus the `deck/` subdir for deck-mode-specific UI.

**Key flow.** Drop EPUB onto shelf → annotations bake on ingest → click book → TOC → chapter renders in page mode with translation under each paragraph + colored annotation underlines → click an underline for popover gloss → toggle to deck mode for one-sentence-at-a-time slow study with arrow keys.

**Brand voice.** Quiet. Serif. The page is the hero — *"everything else bows to the prose"*. The closest thing to a reference design in the fleet.

---

## 2. Overlap & tension matrix

| Concept | podcast-app | learn-stations | flashcard | readalong | book-app |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Audio playback** | KarottenPlayerV3 (heavy) | PlayTTSButton / PlaySlowTTSButton | — | drill recording dot + replay | per-sentence playback in deck mode |
| **Word highlight during audio** | kt3 `data-word-idx` ranges with orange bg | `--tts-highlight-bg` (goldenrod) | — | drill word-chip teal | (planned for chapter listening) |
| **Correct / wrong feedback** | — | `--correct-*` / `--wrong-*` 9-token system | — | `--drill-success/error` gradient | — |
| **Dictionary popover** | — | `EtymologyBubble` | full-card | (separate `/dictionary` route, broken) | `AnnotationPopover` (the best impl) |
| **Progress bar** | timeline scrubber | `DrillDensityBadge` + station counter | — | `--drill-progress-fill` (linear gradient) | chapter percent in nav |
| **Card surface** | episode card on `/` | station card | flashcard | drill card (glassy) | book card on shelf |
| **Type system** | Inter (kt3 font loader) | Plex Sans + JP serif | inherits depot | system sans | **Plex Serif 19/1.75 + Plex Sans** |
| **Theme system** | hardcoded dark | 4 WCAG-locked themes via `[data-theme]` | inherits depot light/dark | tailwindcss-animate + ShadCN `hsl(var(--…))` | light + dark via prefers-color-scheme only |
| **Empty state** | "Pick a podcast" | mascot + course list | "loading…" | drop-zone | drop-zone (same idea) |
| **Settings entry** | none | `SettingsButton` | none | profile/admin pages | mode-toggle + lang-toggle |
| **Auth** | none (open) | next-auth 5 | inherits depot | Firebase (!) | none |
| **Where TTS calls go** | depot `/api/tts` | depot `/api/tts` | (n/a) | client SpeechSynthesis (forbidden) → must migrate | depot `/api/tts` via MS Cognitive |

### 2.1 Unnecessary divergence (things to merge)

1. **Two correct/wrong palettes.** `learn-stations` `--correct-*` (olive `#4a5d00` solid) vs `readalong` `--drill-success` (hsl 142). Pick one — **adopt learn-stations**, it has the AA tests.
2. **Three audio buttons.** `KarottenPlayerV3` orange play, `PlayTTSButton`, `--drill-recording-dot`. Collapse to one `<AudioButton>` primitive with `variant="hero|inline|record"`.
3. **Three "card" surfaces.** Episode card, station card, drill card, book card, flashcard. Same `<Card>` primitive, different content.
4. **Two dictionary popovers.** `EtymologyBubble` (learn-stations) and `AnnotationPopover` (book-app). The book-app one is better-typed and better-named. **Adopt book-app's.**
5. **Two type stacks.** Inter (podcast) vs Plex (book + depot). **Adopt Plex.** It's already in depot/notes globals and book-app uses it. Drop Inter from podcast-app's kt3 loader.
6. **Two dark mode strategies.** `[data-theme]` attribute (learn-stations, notes) vs `prefers-color-scheme` (book-app) vs `.dark` class (readalong). **Adopt `[data-theme]`** — it's testable, user-toggleable, and survives system-theme overrides.
7. **Firebase in readalong.** Migrate to depot SQLite + next-auth before the redesign ships.

### 2.2 Earned moats (do NOT erase)

| App | Keep verbatim | Why |
|---|---|---|
| podcast-app | KarottenPlayerV3 hero scale + scrolling karaoke transcript | Hero player is the brand of the podcast surface. Big, orange, undeniable. |
| learn-stations | Token system + Station discriminated union + zod schema + WCAG test gate | This is the only place type safety + accessibility are enforced. Lift to the shared library. |
| flashcard | LoRA-generated illustration as the front of the card | The illustration *is* the value prop; can't substitute generic clipart. |
| readalong | "Drill" warm-cream + teal palette as a *mode* of the unified drill | User has stated 10× this is the look they love. Make it the default of the drill theme; ship as one of the four named themes. |
| book-app | Plex Serif 19/1.75 reading rhythm + `AnnotationPopover` + deck-mode 460 ms ease-out + drop-zone empty state | Most-refined design surface in fleet. The reference. |

---

## 3. Unified information architecture

### 3.1 Domain & top-level nav

**Two domains, three products under each.**

```
reeloo.ai             (public, Vercel, the "store front")
  /                   home → today's quick-start
  /listen             podcast catalog + hero player
  /read               readalong + book-app (linked: book.reeloo.ai for full-book)
  /learn              learn-stations courses
  /practice           flashcards + daily review (cross-surface dashboard)

internal.reeloo.ai    (workshop, Caddy + minis, agent fleet)
  /workshop           agent dashboard, wizard, content authoring
  /dictionary         dictionary admin + LoRA gallery
  /diary, /inbox      ops surfaces
```

The user-facing nav reduces five apps to **four verbs**: *listen / read / learn / practice*. "Practice" is the cross-app aggregator (today's flashcards + today's drills + today's listening minutes); the other three are the three primary content surfaces.

A 5th product (the **review hub**, currently scattered) becomes a card on `/` rather than its own top-level route. The home page is the daily mission: *3 cards from yesterday's reading, 1 podcast snippet to re-listen, 1 station to clear*.

### 3.2 Cross-app data primitives

Unify on **one** card / item record. Today there are separate schemas in `data/courses`, `data/demos`, `data/books`, `data/syllabus`, plus the live dictionary. Proposal:

```ts
// Shared by all five surfaces; lives in shared-components/ or a new
// @reeloo/data package consumed by both binaries.
type Item =
  | { kind: 'word';      id: string; lang: 'de'|'ja'|'en'|'zh'; word: string; entry: DictEntry; illustrationUrl?: string }
  | { kind: 'sentence';  id: string; lang: string; text: string; translations: Record<string,string>; audioUrl?: string; alignment?: WordTiming[] }
  | { kind: 'episode';   id: string; slug: string; lang: string; title: string; audioUrl: string; transcript: Sentence[] }
  | { kind: 'station';   id: string; courseId: string; unitId: number; variant: StationVariant; payload: unknown /* zod-typed per variant */ }
  | { kind: 'chapter';   id: string; bookId: string; title: string; paragraphs: Paragraph[]; annotations: Annotation[] };

type Annotation = { type: 'vocab'|'grammar'|'idiom'|'place'|'archaism'|'proper-noun'; range: [number,number]; gloss: string; itemRef?: string };

type ReviewItem = { itemId: string; addedAt: number; lastSeen?: number; ease: number; due: number };
```

`Annotation.itemRef` is the join: highlight a word in a book → it points to an `Item.kind='word'` → that's what the flashcard renders → that's what the daily review schedules → that's what learn-stations can pull into a drill. **One review queue. One dictionary. One audio asset table.** Per-app schemas survive only inside the per-app `payload`.

### 3.3 Shared dictionary API

Already live (per `reference_shared_dictionary_api.md`):
`https://dictionary.reeloo.ai/api/dictionary?word=X&lang=de|en|ja`

All five surfaces consume this — no per-app JSON re-reads. CORS=*, normalization built-in. Audio for each entry lives at `/api/tts?text=…&lang=…&voice=…&studio=true`.

### 3.4 Shared media plane

GCS buckets stay public-read:
- `reeloo-podcast-audio` — episode mp3s
- `reeloo-podcast-transcripts` — alignment JSON
- `reeloo-tts-cache` — pre-baked per-word/sentence TTS (Google Wavenet / Studio, never SpeechSynthesis)
- `reeloo-book-audio` — per-chapter mp3 + alignment (book-app's MS Cognitive output)
- `reeloo-illustrations` — LoRA outputs for flashcards + station illustrations

Vercel and Caddy reverse-proxy these via signed URLs only when needed; default is direct browser fetch from the public bucket.

---

## 4. Visual design system

### 4.1 Themes (4 — all must pass WCAG AA at 4.5:1 normal, 3:1 UI)

| Theme key | Anchor | Persona |
|---|---|---|
| `solarized-light` | parchment, base3 `#fdf6e3` | the default. Learn-stations heritage. Adult textbook. |
| `solarized-dark` | base03 `#002b36` | night reading, plane mode |
| `paper` (the readalong palette) | warm cream `hsl(30,30%,92%)` + teal accent `hsl(170,45%,35%)` | drill mode default, cozy |
| `ink` | near-black `#0f1419` + orange `#ff6f3c` (Karotten) | podcast hero |

A theme is selected by user once and persisted in localStorage + per-user profile. Each top-level surface can declare a *theme preference* — `/listen` defaults to `ink`, `/learn` to `solarized-light`, `/read` to `solarized-light` (book reader), `/practice` to `paper`. User can override globally.

### 4.2 Color palette (semantic tokens — same names across themes)

```css
/* surfaces */
--surface              /* page bg */
--surface-container    /* cards / raised */
--surface-elevated     /* modals / sticky toolbars */
--surface-inverse      /* for highlighted blocks against page */

/* text */
--text                 /* default body */
--text-muted           /* secondary */
--text-heading
--text-on-accent       /* foreground when on --accent fill */

/* accents (one primary, one secondary) */
--accent               /* primary CTA bg */
--accent-fg            /* primary CTA text */
--accent-soft          /* hover/pill bg */
--accent-2             /* secondary highlight */

/* graded state */
--correct-bg / --correct-fg / --correct-ring / --correct-solid-bg / --correct-solid-fg
--wrong-bg / --wrong-fg / --wrong-ring / --wrong-solid-bg / --wrong-solid-fg
--warning-bg / --warning-fg / --warning-ring

/* annotation underlines (book-app heritage) */
--anno-vocab / --anno-grammar / --anno-idiom / --anno-place / --anno-archaism / --anno-proper-noun

/* POS coloring (learn-stations heritage, grammar mode) */
--pos-place / --pos-subject / --pos-object / --pos-verb / --pos-particle

/* TTS karaoke word highlight */
--tts-highlight-bg / --tts-highlight-fg / --tts-highlight-ring

/* borders / dividers */
--border / --border-strong / --focus-ring
```

Every theme defines **every** token. The token JSON the design team ships becomes the source of truth — `tokens/solarized-light.json`, `tokens/solarized-dark.json`, `tokens/paper.json`, `tokens/ink.json`. A build step generates `globals.css` `[data-theme="…"]` blocks. A vitest test fails the build on any AA regression (port the existing `lib/design-tokens.test.ts`).

### 4.3 Typography

- **Display & body sans:** IBM Plex Sans (already in depot + book-app). Weights: 400 / 500 / 600 / 700.
- **Reading serif:** IBM Plex Serif. Weights: 400 / 600. Italic 400.
- **Mono:** IBM Plex Mono (for code, debug panels, token tables). Weight 400 / 500.
- **JP serif fallback:** Noto Serif JP for ruby/furigana contexts (learn-stations heritage).
- **DE fraktur (optional, archaism token):** none in scope.

Scale (rem):
```
display-xl   3.5    (book chapter heroes only)
display-lg   2.5    (page hero)
display-md   2.0
display-sm   1.5
title-lg     1.25
title-md     1.125
body-lg      1.125  (book page mode, line-height 1.75)
body         1.0    (default, line-height 1.55)
body-sm      0.875
caption      0.75
mono         0.875
```

### 4.4 Spacing — 4px base

`0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 6 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 24 / 32 / 48 / 64 / 96` × 4px. Component padding defaults: button `8 12`, card `16 20`, modal `24 32`, page gutter `24` mobile / `48` desktop.

### 4.5 Border radius

```
xs   2     (chip outlines)
sm   6     (input, small button)
md   10    (default card)
lg   16    (hero card, modal)
xl   24    (sheet, drawer)
full 9999  (avatar, pill)
```

### 4.6 Shadow scale (paper theme assumes soft, ink theme assumes none)

```
shadow-1   0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.04)
shadow-2   0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.06)
shadow-3   0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,.08)   /* default card */
shadow-4   0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,.10)   /* modal */
shadow-glass  0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,.08), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.5)   /* drill paper-theme card */
```

Each theme overrides — `ink` mostly drops shadow for borders.

### 4.7 Motion

```
duration-instant   80ms    (tap response)
duration-fast      160ms   (hover, focus, chip toggle)
duration-base      240ms   (card expand, popover open)
duration-slow      460ms   (deck-mode crossfade — book-app heritage)
duration-page      640ms   (route-level)

ease-out           cubic-bezier(.2, .8, .2, 1)   (default)
ease-in            cubic-bezier(.4, 0, 1, 1)
ease-out-back      cubic-bezier(.34, 1.56, .64, 1)   (success bounce)
```

Reduced-motion: every animation longer than `duration-fast` checks `prefers-reduced-motion` and falls back to a fade.

### 4.8 Dark mode

**Yes, system-aware *and* user-toggleable**, via `[data-theme]`. System preference seeds the default (light→`solarized-light`, dark→`solarized-dark`) but a user toggle in the global header overrides. Persisted in `localStorage('reeloo:theme')` + synced to user profile on auth. **Drop `.dark` class and `prefers-color-scheme`-only.**

---

## 5. Component inventory (16 primitives)

Each one converges existing per-app variants. Name / purpose / what should converge.

1. **`<Page>`** — top-level surface (header + footer + theme `[data-theme]`). Replaces every per-app layout shell.
2. **`<Card>`** — `variant: 'episode'|'station'|'flashcard'|'book'|'drill'`. Replaces `BookCard`, `RecentRow`, `Station`, the flashcard `<article>`, the drill chip-grid surface.
3. **`<AudioButton>`** — `variant: 'hero'|'inline'|'record'`. `hero` = KarottenPlayerV3-scale orange/teal play button. `inline` = `PlayTTSButton`. `record` = drill's recording-dot button.
4. **`<TranscriptLine>`** — one line of synced text with `data-word-idx` ranges; ties `--tts-highlight-bg`. Replaces kt3 line element + book-app paragraph element + readalong word-chip grid.
5. **`<DictionaryPopover>`** — anchored popover over an underlined span (or a word). Adopts book-app `AnnotationPopover` API; absorbs `EtymologyBubble`. Type chip + gloss + audio button + "save to review" CTA.
6. **`<AnnotationUnderline>`** — wraps a span with one of the 6 `--anno-*` types. Book-app heritage. New in podcast/learn/flashcard contexts.
7. **`<GradedFeedback>`** — `state: 'correct'|'wrong'|'warning'|'idle'` background + ring + optional inline message. Wraps station BottomCTA, drill answer chips, flashcard self-rating row.
8. **`<DrillCard>`** — the warm-cream/teal `paper`-theme card for sentence/word/match drills. Sentence top, chips bottom, progress dot above.
9. **`<Chip>`** — `variant: 'selectable'|'pos'|'level'|'tag'`. POS variant draws from `--pos-*`. Selectable handles match-the-pair and word-bank flows.
10. **`<ProgressBar>`** — linear, with `--accent` fill. Variant `gradient` uses `--drill-progress-fill` (linear-gradient). Replaces the kt3 timeline scrubber's inner bar + drill bar + chapter percentage.
11. **`<Timeline>`** — scrubber for hero audio player (book chapter or podcast episode). Composes `<ProgressBar>` plus a knob and time labels.
12. **`<Mascot>`** — illustrated character in corner. Learn-stations heritage. Now optional per surface (off by default in `/read`).
13. **`<TopNav>`** — global header with `Listen / Read / Learn / Practice` + theme toggle + user menu. Replaces every per-app header.
14. **`<Sheet>`** — bottom-sheet on mobile, side-drawer on desktop. Used by settings, dictionary detail, deck-mode transitions.
15. **`<DropZone>`** — empty-state file-target. Book-app heritage. Reused for "drop an EPUB", "drop an audio file" (workshop only), maybe drag-from-clipboard for new flashcards.
16. **`<TodayDashboard>`** — homepage strip + `/practice` hero. Cards: `Resume reading`, `Today's podcast minute`, `N words due`, `Continue [course]`. New component, no per-app heritage; this is the *connective tissue* the unification creates.

(Stretch, optional: `<RubyText>`, `<JpPosColored>`, `<DrillDensityBadge>`, `<TierPicker>`, `<KaraokeWord>`, `<FlagIssue>` — keep their existing implementations, document them, but they don't need re-design.)

---

## 6. Critical user flows

### 6.1 "I'm reading a book and don't know a word"

1. User opens `book.reeloo.ai/read/<id>` in page mode.
2. Word `verschwommen` carries `<AnnotationUnderline type="vocab">` (the `--anno-vocab` blue solid 2px).
3. Tap → `<DictionaryPopover>` anchored under the word. Type chip = "vocab". Body = primary gloss + IPA + one example sentence.
4. Popover has three actions: `Hear it` (calls `/api/tts`, plays via `<AudioButton variant="inline">`), `Save for review` (POST to `/api/review-queue` — appends the `Item.kind='word'` to user's queue), `See more` (opens `<Sheet>` with full dictionary entry).
5. Save → toast: "Saved. 12 words due today." Toast deep-links to `/practice`.

### 6.2 "I'm listening to a podcast and want to know what just got said"

1. User on `reeloo.ai/listen/<slug>`, hero player playing.
2. KarottenPlayerV3 highlights words as they're spoken (`--tts-highlight-bg`).
3. Tap on highlighted word → audio pauses, `<DictionaryPopover>` appears.
4. Same three actions. Save → adds `{ kind:'word', word:'…' }` *and* `{ kind:'sentence', text:'…', audioUrl:'<deeplink>' }` so the user can later replay the actual line they heard, not a TTS facsimile.
5. Dismiss → audio resumes from `currentTime` (no jump).

### 6.3 "Daily review"

1. User opens `reeloo.ai/practice` (the unified dashboard).
2. `<TodayDashboard>` shows three cards: `Words due (12)`, `Drill (Minna L5)`, `Listen (5 min Karotten)`.
3. Tap `Words due`. Enters a queue of `Item.kind='word'` items.
4. Each one renders `<DrillCard variant="flashcard">` with the LoRA illustration on the front + `<AudioButton variant="inline">` for pronunciation.
5. Three buttons under: `Again` / `Hard` / `Good` (3-button SM-2). Each updates the `ReviewItem` and advances.
6. End of queue → confetti row + "Reading you saved from: *Der Vorleser* — keep going?" (returns to book where words were captured).

### 6.4 "I'm doing a learn-stations drill and want to read more about a grammar point"

1. User on `reeloo.ai/learn/minna/l5`. Station 4 is a particle-fill: 「私\_\_ パンを 食べます」.
2. User gets it wrong twice → `<GradedFeedback state="wrong">` + new affordance: `Read the rule (30 s)` button.
3. Tap → `<Sheet>` slides in with a grammar-point explainer pulled from a *book-app annotation* (so the explainer prose itself is sourced from a curated grammar book in the library — same surface, same `<AnnotationPopover>`-class popover for any words in the explainer).
4. Close sheet → return to station, hint pill above the answer field: "は marks topic. Try again."
5. Get it right → `<GradedFeedback state="correct">` solid-bg green for 1.2s → advance.

### 6.5 (Discovered) "Capture an EPUB I just downloaded, get a 30-minute personalized course out of it"

This is the **dream loop** the unified system enables and no current app does end-to-end.

1. User drops `Goethe-B1.epub` on `reeloo.ai/library` `<DropZone>`.
2. Server: ingest (book-app pipeline) → annotate (Vertex AI) → extract top-50 unseen words → bake LoRA illustrations + TTS audio for each → emit a `course` definition (`Item.kind='station'` discriminated union).
3. User sees a toast: "Course ready: *Der Vorleser* — 5 units, 30 min." Deep-link to `/learn/der-vorleser`.
4. Each unit is auto-generated stations: dictionary popovers for the 10 hardest words → cloze on sentences containing them → listening drill on TTS of the same sentences → final freeform write-back.
5. After each unit, words are queued to `/practice` daily review.

This is the moat. The five apps were each one piece of this. Unify them and you ship the loop.

---

## 7. Mockup deliverables (what we want from Claude design)

Mockups requested at desktop (≥1280) **and** mobile (375):

1. `reeloo.ai/` — home / today dashboard
2. `reeloo.ai/listen` — podcast catalog grid
3. `reeloo.ai/listen/<slug>` — hero player + scrolling transcript (ink theme)
4. `reeloo.ai/read` — library shelf + drop-zone
5. `reeloo.ai/read/<book>/<chapter>` — page mode (solarized-light, Plex Serif)
6. `reeloo.ai/read/<book>/<chapter>` — deck mode (single sentence, 460 ms in)
7. `reeloo.ai/learn` — course picker
8. `reeloo.ai/learn/<course>/<unit>` — station drill (paper theme, 3 station variants visible: cloze, match, pronounce)
9. `reeloo.ai/practice` — daily-review dashboard
10. `reeloo.ai/practice/words` — flashcard review with LoRA illustration
11. `<DictionaryPopover>` — anchored under a word, 3 states (collapsed / expanded / loading TTS)
12. `<GradedFeedback>` — correct / wrong / warning side-by-side
13. `<TopNav>` — light + dark + collapsed mobile drawer
14. `<Sheet>` — mobile bottom-sheet (settings) + desktop side-drawer (grammar explainer)
15. `<DropZone>` — empty + hover + uploading + error states
16. **Theme set:** the same `<Page>` rendered in all four themes (solarized-light / solarized-dark / paper / ink) — for token validation
17. **Component sheet:** all 16 primitives on one page, like a Figma library inventory
18. **Migration map:** per source surface, an annotated screenshot showing "this becomes that"

Deliverables expected back:

1. `tokens.json` — full 4-theme token file, ready to drop into `~/work/notes/lib/design-tokens/`. Includes contrast pairs documented as comments.
2. Component spec sheet — for each of the 16 primitives, named CSS variables + example JSX + Figma frame URL.
3. Figma library file — components mapped via `figma-code-connect` to the actual component file paths above.
4. 18 mockup frames (desktop + mobile) — at minimum as PNG exports, ideally as Figma node URLs we can revisit.
5. Migration map markdown (or annotated mockup set) — for each of the five source apps, "delete / keep / rename / restyle".
6. Two reference screenshots per theme, showing the same page at AA-passing contrast.

**Out of scope for this design pass:**
- Marketing / landing pages for any sub-domain.
- Pricing / billing / checkout flows.
- Workshop pages (`internal.reeloo.ai/*`) — those are agent ops, different audience.
- The wizard form (`/listen/wizard`) — that's an upcoming separate brief on episode authoring.
- Mobile native (Flutter) — the readalong Flutter app stays as-is; we ship web first.
- Localization strings beyond what's already in `learn-stations/messages/` (en, de, ja, zh-TW). No new locale additions.
- Agent fleet status / depot diary / mailbox — workshop only.

---

## 8. Constraints & non-negotiables

These come from explicit user memory and must be honored in any mockup:

1. **Chinese is Traditional only (zh-TW / 繁體中文).** No Simplified anywhere, ever.
2. **TTS quality.** Pre-baked Google Wavenet / Studio or MS Cognitive only. No browser `SpeechSynthesis`. The readalong migration must drop its current client-side TTS.
3. **STT auto-stop.** No "Stop" button. Combine confidence threshold + silence VAD + max duration. The recording-dot UI in `<AudioButton variant="record">` reflects this: it animates while listening, fades when auto-stopped.
4. **WCAG AA enforced at build.** Port `learn-stations/lib/design-tokens.test.ts` to the shared package. Every theme × every (fg, bg) pair tested.
5. **Reading rhythm.** Book page mode: Plex Serif 19px / 1.75 / 720px max-width. Locked.
6. **Drill animation pattern.** Substitution drills cycle the slot, NOT the whole sentence. Matched value slides up + goes green; next slot slides in from bottom. (Memory: `reference_readalong_drill_patterns.md`.)
7. **Solarized-light is the floor.** Any new theme must pass AA on the same component set.
8. **No reliance on light-mode for serif clarity.** Plex Serif body in `solarized-dark` and `ink` must still cite tested fg/bg contrast in tokens.json comments.

---

## 9. Migration map (high-level)

| Source | Action | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `podcast-app/components/KarottenPlayerV3.tsx` | **Move** to `shared-components/AudioHero/` and rename `<AudioHero>` (or keep `KarottenPlayerV3` as a brand-name alias for the `ink` theme). | Drop the scoped style block; rewire to design tokens. |
| `podcast-app/app/page.tsx` | **Replace** with `reeloo.ai/listen` in monorepo. | Catalog → `<Card variant="episode">` grid. |
| `learn-stations` whole repo | **Merge** into `~/work/notes/` under `app/learn/`. Keep zod schemas + station renderers verbatim; rewire tokens to shared. | Largest migration. Keep WCAG test gate. |
| `notes/app/practice/flashcards/page.tsx` | **Keep**, restyle to `<DrillCard variant="flashcard">`. | Add SM-2 row using `<GradedFeedback>`. |
| `readalong/nextjs/src/app/drill/*` | **Port** drill engine to `app/read/<book>/drill/` in monorepo; *delete* every other route from readalong (Firebase, /game, /scenario-sim, /map — dead). | Migrate user data from Firebase to depot SQLite before cutover. |
| `book-app` whole repo | **Stay separate**, link `@reeloo/design-system` as workspace dep. | Adopt new tokens.json. Keep `read.reeloo.ai` ⇄ `book.reeloo.ai` deep links. |
| `notes/app/podcast/_components/PodcastPlayerV3.tsx` | **Delete** after `<AudioHero>` lands. | Duplicate of podcast-app's. |
| Per-app `globals.css` | **Delete**. One `globals.css` per binary, importing `@reeloo/design-system/themes/*.css`. |  |

---

## 10. Recommended next steps after this brief lands

1. Design team consumes this and returns the 6 deliverable items above.
2. Build team (agents under orchestrator) creates `~/work/shared-components/design-system/` as a workspace package, drops `tokens.json` in, generates `globals.css` per theme.
3. Each of the 16 primitives gets one PR that converges its variants. Visual snapshot gate at the design-system package level (Playwright `toHaveScreenshot` against the 18 mockups).
4. Per-app PRs (one per migration row) flip the imports.
5. Final unification PR: route monorepo `reeloo.ai/{listen,read,learn,practice}`, deprecate `podcast.reeloo.ai`, redirect.

ETA at fleet-typical velocity: 3–4 weeks of agent-night cycles + 1 design week.

---

## 11. Screenshots — current state (2026-05-19)

Below: how the five surfaces look *today*, before unification. Each is at the home/entry route. Inconsistency is the point — same user, five visual identities.

### 11.4 readalong / depot library (`localhost:3000/library`)

---

## 12. Appendix — files & paths referenced

```
~/work/podcast-app/                       — Next 16, port 3002, podcast.reeloo.ai
~/work/learn-stations/                    — Next 16, port 3210, drives depot /learn/*
~/work/book-app/                          — Next 16, book.reeloo.ai
~/work/readalong/nextjs/                  — mixed Vite + Next, drives depot /podcast/_components
~/work/notes/app/practice/flashcards/     — inside depot monorepo, /practice/flashcards
~/work/notes/app/podcast/                 — inside depot monorepo, /podcast/*
~/work/notes/app/learn/                   — inside depot monorepo, /learn/* (driven by learn-stations)
~/work/notes/app/library/                 — depot library page (entry to read)
~/work/notes/lib/design-tokens.test.ts    — WCAG AA gate (port to shared package)
~/work/shared-components/                 — existing workspace package; target for design-system/
dictionary.reeloo.ai/api/dictionary       — shared dictionary API
GCS reeloo-podcast-audio                  — public bucket, episode mp3s
GCS reeloo-tts-cache                      — pre-baked Wavenet/Studio TTS
GCS reeloo-illustrations                  — LoRA-trained flashcard art
```

**End of brief.**
